user_query:
I’m recording my conservatory pre-screen at home and want to confirm my laptop is ready for a clean take. Please do a local system readiness check and produce three outputs under the workspace:

1) outputs/system_status.json — A structured machine-readable report that:
   - Reads input/recording_requirements.yaml and uses those thresholds.
   - Captures measured system info: os.name, os.version, cpu.model, cpu.cores_logical, memory.total_gb, memory.available_gb, disk.mount (the workspace or home volume), disk.total_gb, disk.free_gb, battery.percent (null if not available), audio.devices_input (list of device names if detectable, else empty), measurements.background_cpu_percent (average over ~5 seconds if measured, else null), measurements.sample_rate_hz (detected if feasible, else null).
   - Includes requirements from the YAML verbatim under requirements.original.
   - Includes a requirements.evaluation object with pass/fail/unknown fields for: min_free_disk_gb, min_total_ram_gb, preferred_os (true if measured OS is in list), max_background_cpu_percent (compare to measured value if not null), and target_sample_rate_hz (compare if a sample rate was detected). If a metric can’t be detected, set its measured value to null and the evaluation to "unknown". Do not require admin rights or any installs.

2) outputs/system_report.txt — A concise, human-readable summary that:
   - States the project name from the YAML.
   - Lists the key measurements (OS, CPU, RAM, free disk, battery if present) and a bullet list of requirement checks with Pass/Fail/Unknown.
   - Ends with one short recommendation (<= 40 words) on whether it’s safe to proceed and any quick prep (e.g., close apps).

3) outputs/message_to_director.txt — Read input/message_draft.txt and rewrite it for clarity, positive tone, and brevity (<= 160 words). Include 2–3 concrete highlights from the system check (e.g., free disk GB, total RAM, overall pass/fail impression). Keep it courteous and student-appropriate.

Inputs you must use:
- input/recording_requirements.yaml (extract thresholds and project name)
- input/message_draft.txt (rewrite and incorporate key facts from your findings)

Notes:
- Use OS-native commands or standard libraries only.
- If any field is unavailable on this machine, output null for the measurement and mark the corresponding evaluation as "unknown" in the JSON.
- All three artifacts must be created for the task to be complete. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
